quotes.text: Thou hast made us for Thyself, and our heart is restless, until it repose in Thee.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. E. B. Pusey (1838), public domain

quotes.text: Too late loved I Thee, O Beauty so old, and so new! Too late loved I Thee!
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. E. B. Pusey (1838), public domain

quotes.text: Let us sing Alleluia here below while we are still anxious, that we may sing it one day above in security.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: after the Benedictine text; NPNF series I, public domain

quotes.text: John is the voice, but the Lord is the Word: the voice passes away, the Word remains.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church

quotes.text: Grace does not destroy nature, but perfects it.
quotes.author: St. Thomas Aquinas
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. Fathers of the English Dominican Province (1911–1925), public domain

quotes.text: In the heart of the Church, my Mother, I will be Love.
quotes.author: St. Thérèse of the Child Jesus
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. T. N. Taylor (1912), public domain

quotes.text: All true and living devotion presupposes the love of God; indeed it is itself a true love of God.
quotes.author: St. Francis de Sales
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church

quotes.text: Would you honour the Body of Christ? Neglect Him not when naked; do not honour Him here in the church with silken garments, while neglecting Him outside, where He is cold and naked.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series I, vol. 10, public domain

quotes.text: Do you fast? Give me proof of it by your works. If you see a poor man, take pity on him.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series I, vol. 9, public domain

quotes.text: Hell took a body, and met God face to face. It took earth, and encountered Heaven.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church

quotes.text: Ignorance of the Scriptures is ignorance of Christ.
quotes.author: St. Jerome
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 6, public domain

quotes.text: The divine words grow with the one who reads them.
quotes.author: St. Gregory the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Doctor of the Church

quotes.text: Let nothing disturb thee, nothing affright thee; all things are passing; God never changeth; patient endurance attaineth to all things; who God possesseth in nothing is wanting; alone God sufficeth.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Jesus (Ávila)
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. H. W. Longfellow (d. 1882), public domain

quotes.text: In the evening of life, thou shalt be examined in love.
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church

quotes.text: If you are what you ought to be, you will set fire to all Italy.
quotes.author: St. Catherine of Siena
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. Vida D. Scudder (1905), public domain

quotes.text: I do not seek to understand that I may believe, but I believe in order to understand.
quotes.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. S. N. Deane (1903), public domain

quotes.text: For He was made man that we might be made God.
quotes.author: St. Athanasius
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: On the Incarnation of the Word
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 4, public domain

quotes.text: The bread which you keep belongs to the hungry; the coat which you guard in your chest belongs to the naked.
quotes.author: St. Basil the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Homily on the words of Luke, 'I will pull down my barns'

quotes.text: For that which He has not assumed He has not healed; but that which is united to His Godhead is also saved.
quotes.author: St. Gregory Nazianzen
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 7, public domain

quotes.text: Yesterday I was crucified with Him; today I am glorified with Him. Yesterday I died with Him; today I am quickened with Him.
quotes.author: St. Gregory Nazianzen
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 7, public domain

quotes.text: Christian, acknowledge thy dignity, and becoming a partner in the Divine nature, refuse to return to the old baseness by degenerate conduct.
quotes.author: St. Leo the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 12, public domain

quotes.text: What the Christian should be doing at all times should now be done with greater care and devotion.
quotes.author: St. Leo the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 12, public domain

quotes.text: The reason for loving God is God Himself; the measure, is to love Him without measure.
quotes.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot and Doctor of the Church

quotes.text: In His first coming He came in the flesh and in weakness; in this middle coming, in spirit and in power; in the last, in glory and in majesty.
quotes.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot and Doctor of the Church

quotes.text: Ask grace rather than instruction, desire rather than understanding, the groaning of prayer rather than the study of reading.
quotes.author: St. Bonaventure
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: The Journey of the Mind to God

quotes.text: He who prays is certainly saved; he who does not pray is certainly lost.
quotes.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: The Great Means of Salvation: Prayer
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. ed. E. Grimm (1886), public domain

quotes.text: Christ is the Morning Star, who, when the night of this world is past, brings to His saints the promise of the light of life, and opens everlasting day.
quotes.author: St. Bede the Venerable
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church

quotes.text: Prayer is the ascent of the mind to God.
quotes.author: St. John Damascene
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 9, public domain

quotes.text: O Lord and Master of my life, give me not the spirit of sloth, of despondency, of ambition, or of idle talking; but bestow on me, Thy servant, the spirit of chastity, of humility, of patience, and of love.
quotes.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian
quotes.authorTitle: Deacon and Doctor of the Church

quotes.text: Since then He Himself declared and said of the Bread, 'This is My Body,' who shall dare to doubt any longer?
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 7, public domain

quotes.text: We preach not one advent only of Christ, but a second also, far more glorious than the former.
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 7, public domain

quotes.text: I am the wheat of God, and let me be ground by the teeth of the wild beasts, that I may be found the pure bread of Christ.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr

quotes.text: For the glory of God is a living man; and the life of man consists in beholding God.
quotes.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop, Martyr and Doctor of the Church

quotes.text: He can no longer have God for his Father, who has not the Church for his mother.
quotes.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.work: On the Unity of the Church

quotes.text: Eighty and six years have I served Him, and He never did me any injury: how then can I blaspheme my King and my Saviour?
quotes.author: St. Polycarp of Smyrna
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr

quotes.text: And on the day called Sunday, all who live in cities or in the country gather together to one place, and the memoirs of the apostles or the writings of the prophets are read.
quotes.author: St. Justin Martyr
quotes.authorTitle: Martyr

quotes.text: What doth it profit thee to enter into deep discussion concerning the Holy Trinity, if thou lack humility, and be thus displeasing to the Trinity?
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Canon Regular

quotes.text: Love is a great thing, a good above all others, which alone maketh every burden light.
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Canon Regular

quotes.text: God has created me to do Him some definite service; He has committed some work to me which He has not committed to another. I have my mission.
quotes.author: St. John Henry Newman
quotes.authorTitle: Cardinal

quotes.text: Christ be with me, Christ within me, Christ behind me, Christ before me, Christ beside me, Christ to win me, Christ to comfort and restore me.
quotes.author: St. Patrick (attributed)
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. C. F. Alexander (1889), public domain

quotes.text: Hearken, O my son, to the precepts of thy master, and incline the ear of thine heart.
quotes.author: St. Benedict of Nursia
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot

quotes.text: The life of a monk ought always to have a Lenten character.
quotes.author: St. Benedict of Nursia
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot

quotes.text: Praised be Thou, my Lord, with all Thy creatures, especially Sir Brother Sun, who is the day, and through whom Thou givest us light.
quotes.author: St. Francis of Assisi
quotes.authorTitle: Founder of the Friars Minor

quotes.text: Take, O Lord, and receive all my liberty, my memory, my understanding, and all my will — all that I have and possess.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Loyola
quotes.authorTitle: Founder of the Society of Jesus

quotes.text: The Christian ideal has not been tried and found wanting. It has been found difficult; and left untried.
quotes.author: G. K. Chesterton
quotes.authorTitle: Author (not canonized)

quotes.text: Love unites us to God; love covers a multitude of sins; love beareth all things, is long-suffering in all things.
quotes.author: St. Clement of Rome
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Martyr

quotes.text: Before the blessing of the heavenly words another species is named; after the consecration the Body is signified.
quotes.author: St. Ambrose of Milan
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 10, public domain
